diagrams.title: TCP Server Client Connection Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the complete flow of establishing a TCP connection between client and server with all system calls involved.
elements: Client calls socket() → creates client socket
Server calls socket() → creates listening socket
Server calls bind() → binds to port
Server calls accept() → blocks for connections
Three-way handshake shown between connect and accept
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the exact sequence of socket calls for TCP communication and understand the relationship between client and server actions.

diagrams.title: Concurrent Server Architecture (Fork-based)
diagrams.description: Illustrates how a concurrent server uses fork() to handle multiple clients simultaneously.
elements: Parent calls accept() → gets client socket
Parent calls fork() → creates child process
Parent closes client socket, continues loop
Multiple child processes shown handling different clients
diagrams.purpose: Understand how forking enables concurrency and the importance of proper file descriptor management.

diagrams.title: Socket Address Structures
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between generic sockaddr and specific address structures like sockaddr_in (IPv4) and sockaddr_in6 (IPv6).
elements: struct sockaddr_in for IPv4 (16 bytes): sin_family, sin_port, sin_addr, sin_zero
diagrams.purpose: Understand socket address structures and the importance of casting when using them with socket functions.
